scopeAnchors.In: Input-arguments object of the current function; In.<Arg> reads an arg.
scopeAnchors.Out: Return-value object of the current function; assign with Out = ....
scopeAnchors.Parent: The parent group of the current object; chain Parent.Parent. to walk further up the group tree.
scopeAnchors.Root: The project root group; start of an absolute object reference.
scopeAnchors.This: The group the current object lives in; disambiguates same-named objects / library names.
scopeAnchors.Library: Forces the library namespace; use when a project object shadows a library name, e.g. Library.Calculate.Max(...).

unsupported.==: use 'eq' (only on Integer or Enumeration; never on Floating Point)
unsupported.while: iteration is not supported; M1 code runs once per scheduled event
unsupported.for: iteration is not supported; use 'expand ... to' for inline repeats

lexing.localDeclaration: local [<Type>] name = value;  (type inferred if omitted)
lexing.staticLocalDeclaration: static local [<Type>] name = value;  (initialiser must be literal/enumerator/constant)
lexing.expandSubstitution: $(name) inside an 'expand (name = a to b) { }' block (pure text substitution, not type-checked)

naming.objects: Begin with an uppercase letter; spaces allowed between words; referenced by parent group (Fuel.Pressure).
naming.locals: Begin with a lowercase letter (camelCase optional); no spaces.
naming.enumerators: Keep short; value 0 = neutral/initial state; negatives for errors, positives for normal operation.

diagnostics.rule: A stateful (purple-icon) library function must not be called conditionally or inside a comparison; call it every execution.
A channel may be assigned at most once per code path (not in two functions, not twice in one). Use if/else or a local instead.
Equality/inequality (eq/neq) is not supported on Floating Point; compare with a tolerance via Calculate.Absolute(a - b) < eps.
Comparisons between signed and unsigned integers are not allowed; use Convert.ToInteger / Convert.ToUnsignedInteger.
A when/is must cover every enumerator of the argument's enum.
Integrated-only objects (BR2, GPS, ...) must not be called directly.
Overload is deprecated; prefer its replacement.
Names must not differ only by case, nor collide with a class or library function name.
Token is not part of M1PL (see 'unsupported' for replacement).

members.name: OK

members.name: Off
members.doc: No output pulse was requested for the last camshaft lobe. Either: •Delivery Angle was after Limit Angle, or •Over Pressure Mode is Enabled.

members.name: Skip
members.doc: A large change in Delivery Angle or camshaft phase resulted in a pump camshaft lobe being skipped because the pulse should have started more than 10° before the current engine position. The change would typically need to be more than 10° and applied just before the pulse was about to be generated.

members.name: Limit

members.name: Normally Open

members.name: Normally Closed
members.doc: The solenoid needs to be switched on to open the intake valve and prevent delivering fuel to the high pressure rail, eg Hitachi Gen III. When the solenoid is disconnected fuel pressure increases to maximum.

members.name: Frequency
members.doc: Measure normalised frequency and duty cycle: •'A' resource reports normalised frequency where 5kHz is 5V. •'B' resource reports normalised duty cycle where 100% is 5V.

members.name: SENT Throttle
members.doc: Measure dual throttle position: •'A' resource reports TPS1: 12bit fast channel 1 with 100% (4095) scaled to 5V.Values from 4.992V (4089) indicate an error. •'B' resource reports TPS2: 12bit fast channel 1 with 100% (4095) scaled to 5V.Values from 4.992V (4089) indicate an error. NOTE: TPS2 is typically the opposite of TPS1 NOTE: Some throttle servos (2017+) transmit position as a secure signal.If the 'B' resource appears to be noisy try Type = SENT Secure Serial. See: SAE J2716 SENT frame format H.1: Two 12-bit fast channels (6 data nibbles) See also: SAE J2716 SENT frame format H.5: Single sensor with 12-bit fast channel 1 and zero value on fast channel 2 (6 data nibbles)

members.name: Pulse Width
members.doc: Measure normalised frequency and pulse width: •'A' resource reports normalised frequency where 5kHz is 5V. •'B' resource reports normalised pulse width where 5ms is 5V.

members.name: SENT Pressure Temperature Serial
members.doc: Dual (redundant) pressure measurements plus serial temperature: •'A' resource reports Pressure 1 (or Pressure 2 if Pressure 1 fails)with sensor specific calibration. •'B' resource reports Temperature, typically with the calibration below (See: SAE J2716 SENT E.2.2.1 Default Linear Temperature Transfer Characteristic Function). Voltage (V) 0.0 5.0 Temperature (°C) -73.15 438.725 See: SAE J2716 SENT frame format H.1: Two 12-bit fast channels (6 data nibbles) andPressure Sensor Type: P/P [$006]

members.name: Hella PWM Oil Pressure
members.doc: Hella OPS+T oil pressure and temperature sensor: •'A' resource reports Oil Pressure with the calibration below: Voltage (V) 0.0 4.845 Pressure (bar) 0.5 10.5 •'B' resource reports Oil Temperature with the calibration below: Voltage (V) 0.0 4.845 Temperature (°C) -40 160

members.name: Hella Oil Level
members.doc: Hella Packaged Ultrasonic Level Sensor (PULS) oil level and temperature sensor. •Examples: ◦VW 06E907660C, 03F907660E, etc. engine oil sensors ◦Honda K20C engine oil sensor •Active Edge: Rising Edge •Pullup Control: On •Threshold: 2.0V •Hysteresis: 0.5V •'A' resource reports Oil Level with 5V representing 1600msec cooling time. Calibrations below: Voltage (V) 0.156 2.859 Level (mm) 120 18 Honda K20C engine sensor calibration (03F907660E ?) Voltage (V) 0.0 2.6 Level (mm) 111 0 06E907660C calibration •'B' resource reports Oil Temperature with 5V representing a 100msec temperature signal,resulting in the calibration below: Voltage (V) 1.25 2.50 4.50 Temperature (°C) -48 50 175

members.name: SENT Secure Serial
members.doc: Secure sensor measurement plus serial temperature: •'A' resource reports 12bit fast channel 1 with 100% (4095) scaled to 5V.Values from 4.992V (4089) indicate an error. •'B' resource may report Temperature with the calibration below. Voltage (V) 0.0 5.0 Temperature (°C) -73.15 438.725 See: SAE J2716 SENT frame format H.4: Secure sensor with 12-bit fast channel 1 and secure sensor information on fast channel 2 (6 data nibbles)

members.name: SENT Secure Serial 18
members.doc: Secure sensor measurement plus serial temperature (18µs tick period): •'A' resource reports 12bit fast channel 1 with 100% (4095) scaled to 5V.Values from 4.992V (4089) indicate an error. •'B' resource may report Temperature with the calibration below. Voltage (V) 0.0 5.0 Temperature (°C) -73.15 438.725 See: SAE J2716 SENT frame format H.4: Secure sensor with 12-bit fast channel 1 and secure sensor information on fast channel 2 (6 data nibbles)

members.name: SENT Single
members.doc: Single sensor measurement: •'A' resource reports 12bit fast channel 1 with 100% (4095) scaled to 5V.Values from 4.992V (4089) indicate an error. •'B' resource reports slow serial sensor value. Magnetic Field Sensor: •Application: Park Lock Position Sensor in the 2020 Chevrolet Corvette C8 dual clutch gearbox. •Part Number: SNS0258F_01. •'A' resource reports a linear value corresponding to magnet position. The range is approximately 0V to 5V •'B' resource reports a value that looks the same as the 'A' resource. See: SAE J2716 SENT frame format H.2: One 12-bit fast channel (3 data nibbles)

members.name: SENT 14bit 10bit
members.doc: Decode linear 14bit data, typically used for MAF sensors: •'A' resource reports 14bit fast channel 1 with 100% (16383) scaled to 5V.Values from 4.998V (16377) indicate an error. •'B' resource reports 10bit fast channel 2 with 100% (1023) scaled to 5V.Values from 4.97V (1017) indicate an error. See: SAE J2716 SENT frame format H.6: Two fast channels with 14-bit fast channel 1 and 10-bit fast channel 2 (6 data nibbles)

members.name: SENT 16bit 8bit
members.doc: Decode linear 16bit data, typically used for MAF sensors: •'A' resource reports 16bit fast channel 1 with 100% (65535) scaled to 5V.Values from 4.9995V (65529) indicate an error. •'B' resource reports 8bit fast channel 2 with 100% (255) scaled to 5V.Values from 4.88V (249) indicate an error. See: SAE J2716 SENT frame format H.7: Two fast channels with 16-bit fast channel 1 and 8-bit fast channel 2 (6 data nibbles)

members.name: Hella PULSIV Oil Level
members.doc: Hella Packaged Ultrasonic Level Sensor (PULSIV) oil level and temperature sensor.This sensor can be identified by a signal containing three positive pulses,repeating once a second. •Examples: ◦PULSIV 150mm, part number: 6PR 015.526-00, as used in the Stellantis Hurricane T6 (inline 6 cylinder, twin turbo) engine •Active Edge: Rising Edge •Pullup Control: On •Threshold: 2.0V •Hysteresis: 0.5V •'A' resource reports Oil Level with 5V representing 100% duty cycle. Calibrations below: Voltage (V) 1.045 3.990 Level (mm) 0 150 PULSIV 150mm calibration (6PR 015.526-00) •'B' resource reports Oil Temperature with 5V representing a 100% duty cycle,resulting in the calibration below: Voltage (V) 1.000 1.045 3.990 4.000 Temperature (°C) RTD short -40 160 RTD broken

members.doc: A Synchronisation input indicated the Cycle Lockposition was incorrect. For safety two engine cycles without a Diagnostic are required before Cycle Lock will resume. Possible causes: the synchronisation input has moved further than is supported by the reference mode

members.name: Missed Tooth
members.doc: The reference input detected a tooth pitch above Wide PitchThreshold, indicating an edge has been missed or reference is not correctly locked to the pattern. The input falls back to searching for alignment with the reference pattern.

members.name: Noise
members.doc: The reference input detected a tooth pitch below Narrow PitchThreshold, indicating an edge has been missed or reference is not correctly locked to the pattern. The input falls back to searching for alignment with the reference pattern.

members.name: Restart
members.doc: The reference calculation was requested to restart. Triggered used when switching to an alternate reference input.

members.name: Skip
members.doc: The reference calculation detected an unexpected error: a tooth edge was missed and the calculations are no longer correctly locked to the reference pattern. The calculation will reset so normal operation can resume.

members.name: Phase
members.doc: The reference calculation detected an unexpected error: the first tooth edge was detected at the wrong position. The calculation will reset so normal operation can resume.

members.name: Timeout
members.doc: The reference input was not updated for 100ms so the reference calculation was shutdown for safety

members.name: Test Active
members.doc: A reference tooth was detected while tests were running so the system was shutdown for safety.

members.name: Experimental Mode
members.doc: Mode is experimental and maximum engine speed is limited to 3000rpm. Please test and help confirm the mode is working correctly so this restriction can be removed. Provide a description of the engine designation and vehicle tested along with data logging that includes several engine starts and variable camshafts, if fitted,moved through the full range. The the channels are required in data logging, all at their maximum supported rates: •Engine Speed Reference, Pin and Voltage channels •Variable cam engines: Camshaft Input, Pin and Voltage channels •Fixed cam engines: Synchronisation Input, Pin and Voltage channels

Sensor details: •Reference: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: Tooth Count teeth per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: edge before the synchronisation Active Edge •Synchronisation: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: one tooth •Inlet and exhaust camshafts: magnetic or hall sensor ◦Tooth pattern: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 or 8 evenly spaced teeth •SIM3 mode: 10 - 1F

members.name: Crankshaft One Missing Four Stroke
members.doc: Supported engines: •Four stroke engine with one missing Reference tooth on crankshaft and one synchronisation tooth. Sensor details: •Reference: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: Tooth Count-1 teeth per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: edge before the synchronisation Active Edge •Synchronisation: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: one tooth ◦Inlet Camshaft Bank 1 or Exhaust Camshaft Bank 1 sensors with 1 tooth are also used for synchronisation •Inlet and exhaust camshafts: magnetic or hall sensor ◦Tooth pattern: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 or 8 evenly spaced teeth •SIM3 mode: 2C, 30, 32, 35, 37

members.name: Crankshaft Two Missing Four Stroke
members.doc: Supported engines: •Four stroke engine with two missing Reference teeth on crankshaft and one synchronisation tooth. •BMW S50B30 Sensor details: •Reference: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: Tooth Count-2 teeth per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: edge before the synchronisation Active Edge •Synchronisation: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: one tooth ◦Inlet Camshaft Bank 1 or Exhaust Camshaft Bank 1 sensors with 1 tooth are also used for synchronisation •Inlet and exhaust camshafts: magnetic or hall sensor ◦Tooth pattern: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6 or 8 evenly spaced teeth •SIM3 mode: 28, 2D, 31, 33, 34, 99

members.name: Camshaft One Missing Four Stroke
members.doc: Supported engines: •Four stroke engine with one missing Reference tooth on the camshaft. •Ducatti motorcycles •Triumph motorcycles Sensor details: •Reference: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: Tooth Count-1 teeth per cycle ◦Cycle lock position: edge before the synchronisation Active Edge •Synchronisation: not required.

members.name: Camshaft Two Missing Four Stroke
members.doc: Supported engines: •Four stroke engine with two missing Reference teeth on the camshaft •Ducatti motorcycles •Triumph motorcycles Sensor details: •Reference: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: Tooth Count-2 teeth per cycle ◦Cycle lock position: edge before the synchronisation Active Edge •Synchronisation: not required. •SIM3 mode 87, 88.

members.name: Multi Tooth Two Stroke
members.doc: Supported engines: •Two stroke engine with evenly spaced Reference teeth on crankshaft and one synchronisation tooth. Sensor details: •Reference: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: Tooth Count teeth per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: edge before the synchronisation Active Edge •Synchronisation: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: one tooth

members.name: Crankshaft One Missing Two Stroke
members.doc: Supported engines: •Two stroke engine with one missing Reference tooth on the crankshaft. Sensor details: •Reference: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: Tooth Count-1 teeth per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: edge before the synchronisation Active Edge •Synchronisation: not required.

members.name: Crankshaft Two Missing Two Stroke
members.doc: Supported engines: •Two stroke engine with two missing Reference teeth on the crankshaft. Sensor details: •Reference: magnetic or hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: Tooth Count-2 teeth per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: edge before the synchronisation Active Edge •Synchronisation: not required.

members.name: Denso 270 90
members.doc: For engines with the Denso camshaft pattern: one 270° wide tooth and one 90° wide tooth. Reference pattern 60-2, ie 57 teeth of 6° pitch and 1 of 18° pitch. The Reference cycle lock position is the wide tooth spanned by the 270° wide camshaft tooth. •Reference: Rising edge. •All camshafts: ◦Used for synchronisation. ◦Camshaft pattern lock position: edge between the 270° and 180° wide regions. ◦Active Edge: Both. Active edge only influences trigger voltages derived from the hysteresis and threshold levels. •Volvo B8444S engine as used in XC90, S80 and Noble M600: ◦Reference Offset: 98°. ◦SIM3 mode: 97. •Jaguar Direct Injection AJ-V8 Gen III (AJ133): ◦Reference Offset: 372°. ◦SIM3 mode: 97.

members.name: Honda Bike Synchronisation
members.doc: For Honda Bike cams with three teeth with pitches 60°, 360°, 300°.The Reference cycle lock position is the tooth edge before the 60° pitch tooth. •Reference Offset: 461°. •One Synchronisation input supported. •SIM3 mode 92. Camshaft edge angles are reported: •The 'Offset' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used. •The 'Smooth' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used.

members.name: Ford Duratec
members.doc: Supported Engines: •Ford Barra straight 6 engine (eg BA Falcon) •Ford Cyclone 3.7L V6 (also branded Duratec) •Reference: magnetic sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: 36-1, ie 34 of 10° pitch and 1 of 20° pitch ◦Cycle lock position: wide tooth before the first 240° pitch camshaft tooth ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: Off ◦Threshold: 0.0V ◦Hysteresis: Engine Speed dependent •Inlet and Exhaust Camshafts: magnetic sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: 240°, 120°, 120°, 240°. ◦Pattern lock position: end of the first 240° pitch camshaft tooth ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: Off ◦Threshold: 0.0V ◦Hysteresis: Engine Speed dependent ◦Bank 1 Inlet zero position: 110° ◦Bank 1 Exhaust zero position: 40° ◦Bank 2 Inlet zero position: 470° (360° after Bank 1) ◦Bank 2 Exhaust zero position: 400° (360° after Bank 1) •Synchronisation: ◦All camshafts used for synchronisation •Reference Offset: 409° (Ford Barra). •SIM3 mode: 3D.

members.name: General Motors LS7
members.doc: Supports General Motors LS7. •Reference: hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: 57 of 6°, 1 of 18° (60-2) per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: wide tooth before the sync pattern lock position ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 2.0V ◦Hysteresis: 1.0V ◦Offset: 439°. •Synchronisation: hall sensor ◦Tooth pattern: 4 evenly spaced tooth: 2 of 30° wide, 2 of 150° wide. ◦Camshaft pattern lock position: edge at the start of the first 30° wide tooth. ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge (both edges used) ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 2.0V ◦Hysteresis: 1.0V •SIM3 mode: 96

members.name: Nissan RB26
members.doc: Nissan RB26 and other six cylinder engines with 360° optical trigger on camshaft that has windows of widths 48°, 40°, 32°, 24°,16°, 8°. The Reference cycle lock position is the reference before the widest sync window in the disc. •Reference and Synchronisation Pins must use identical settings: ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 0.8V ◦Hysteresis: 0.2V ◦Debounce: 4µs •One synchronisation input supported. •Reference Offset approximately 112°. •SIM3 Mode 46. Camshaft edge angles are reported: •The 'Offset' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used. •The 'Smooth' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used.

members.name: Nissan SR20
members.doc: Nissan SR20, CA18DET and other four cylinder engines with 360° optical trigger on camshaft that has windows of widths 32°,24°, 16°, 8°. The reference cycle lock position is the reference before the widest sync window in the disc. •Reference and Synchronisation Pins must use identical settings: ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 0.8V ◦Hysteresis: 0.2V ◦Debounce: 4µs •One synchronisation input supported. •Reference Offset: (information required)°. •SIM3 mode 43. Camshaft edge angles are reported: •The 'Offset' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used. •The 'Smooth' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used.

members.name: Nissan One wide slot
members.doc: Nissan RB30 and other engines with 360° optical trigger on camshaft that has one window of width 16° and multiple windows of width 4°. The reference cycle lock position is the reference before the widest sync window in the disc. •Reference and Synchronisation Pins must use identical settings: ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 0.8V ◦Hysteresis: 0.2V ◦Debounce: 4µs •One synchronisation input supported. •Reference Offset: (information required)°. •4 Cylinder SIM3 mode 42. •6 Cylinder SIM3 mode 44. Camshaft edge angles are reported: •The 'Offset' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used. •The 'Smooth' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used.

members.name: Bosch 140 40
members.doc: Supported Engines: •General Motors LLT (direct injection 60° V6) •Audi BXA / Lamborghini LP560 (direct injection 90° V10) •Mazda L3-VDT (2.3L DISI Turbo) •Ferrari F430 •Mercedes M156 and M159 (6.2L DOHC V8, eg CLK63, SLS) •Reference: ◦Tooth pattern: 57 of 6°, 1 of 18° (60-2) per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: wide tooth before the 40°, 40° region on all camshafts ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge •Inlet and exhaust camshafts: ◦Tooth pattern: 40°/140°, 40°/140°, 140°/40°, 140°/40° (40° tolerance). ◦Camshaft pattern lock position: edge between the two 40° wide regions. ◦Active Edge: Both. Active edge only influences trigger voltages derived from the hysteresis and threshold levels. ◦All camshafts must have the 40°, 40° cam region in the same region of the crankshaft pattern, ie after the same missing crank teeth. •Synchronisation: ◦Dedicated sensor and / or camshafts •General Motors LLT: ◦Reference Offset: 440.5°. ◦SIM3 mode 96. •Audi BXA / Lamborghini LP560: ◦Reference Offset: 72°. ◦SIM3 mode 9C. •Mazda L3-VDT: ◦Reference Offset: 113°. ◦SIM3 mode 7F. •Mercedes M156 and M159 ◦Reference Offset: 59°. See also the Bosch 140 40 Alternate Reference Mode.

members.name: Subaru EJ20G
members.doc: Subaru GC8 WRX and STi (eg EJ20G, EJ20K, EJ207) from MY95 - MY00. Reference teeth 55°, 93°, 32°. The reference cycle lock position is the 55° pitch tooth before the double narrow pitch (three tooth) region on the cam. •Reference: Falling edge. •Synchronisation: Falling edge. •Reference Offset: 65.0°. •One synchronisation input supported. •SIM3 mode 64. Camshaft edge angles are reported: •The 'Offset' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used. •The 'Smooth' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used.

members.name: Honda K20A
members.doc: Supported Engines: •Honda K20A (eg Honda Civic Type R, Honda Integra Type R). Sensor details: •Reference: hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: 1 of 20°, 11 of 30°, 1 of 10° (12+1) per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: extra Reference tooth near the extra Synchronisation tooth ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 2.0V ◦Hysteresis: 1.0V •Synchronisation: hall sensor ◦Tooth pattern: 4+1 ◦Active Edge: Rising Edge ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 3.0V ◦Hysteresis: 1.0V •Inlet Camshaft Bank 1: hall sensor ◦Tooth pattern: 4 even teeth ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 2.0V ◦Hysteresis: 1.0V •Reference Offset: -36°. •SIM3 mode: 2A. K20C engines (2015+) require the Honda K20C1 Reference Mode.

members.name: Fiat TwinAir
members.doc: Fiat 500 TwinAir. Reference pattern 60-2, ie 57 teeth of 6° pitch and 1 of 18° pitch. The reference cycle lock position is the end of the 18° pitch tooth near the narrow camshaft teeth. Camshaft input uses both edges. Active edge only influences trigger voltages derived from the hysteresis and threshold levels. •Reference Offset: 102° (unverified). •One Synchronisation input supported. •SIM3 mode 9a. Camshaft edge angles are reported: •The 'Offset' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used. •The 'Smooth' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used.

members.name: Nissan VQ35
members.doc: Nissan VQ35HR engine Nissan VR38DETT engine as used in the R35 GTR 2007 -. Reference teeth 9 of 10°, 1 of 30° repeated 3 times. The reference cycle lock position is the end of the 30° pitch tooth closest to the edge between the 120° and 33° pitch teeth. •Reference: Falling edge. •Camshaft: Falling edge. •Both inlet camshafts used for synchronisation. •Inlet camshaft pattern lock position: edge between the 120° and 33° pitch teeth. •Position measurement for VQ35HR exhaust camshafts. •VQ35HR Reference Offset: 300°. •VR38DETT Reference Offset: -160°. •SIM3 mode: 91.

members.name: BMW N55
members.doc: Supported engines: •N52, N55 straight 6 •N74 V12 •Reference: hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: 57 of 6°, 1 of 18° (60-2) per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: wide tooth in the 180° high camshaft region ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 2.0V ◦Hysteresis: 1.0V ◦Offset: 60°. •Inlet and exhaust camshafts: hall sensor ◦Tooth pitch pattern: 48° low, 132° high, 180° low, 42° high, 138° low, 180° high ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge (both edges used) ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 2.0V ◦Hysteresis: 1.0V ◦Inlet zero position: 90° ◦Exhaust zero position: 93° •Synchronisation: ◦All camshafts are used for synchronisation •SIM3 mode: 70

members.name: Scania SGL12A
members.doc: Various engines detailed below: •Scania SGL12A: ◦Reference: magnetic sensor. ◾Tooth pattern: 57 of 6°, 1 of 18° (60-2) per revolution ◾Cycle lock position: wide tooth before the extra synchronisation tooth ◾Active Edge: Falling Edge ◾Pullup Control: Off ◾Threshold: 0.0V ◾Hysteresis: Engine Speed dependent ◾Offset: 55°. ◦Synchronisation: magnetic sensor ◾Tooth pitch pattern: 6+1: extra tooth is 30° after an evenly spaced tooth ◾Active Edge: Falling Edge ◾Pullup Control: Off ◾Threshold: 0.0V ◾Hysteresis: Engine Speed dependent ◦SIM3 mode: 9B. •Agco Sisu Power 84G: ◦Reference Offset: 180°. •Cummins ISX Diesel Engine: ◦Reference Offset: (information required)°.

members.name: Subaru EJ207AVCS
members.doc: Supported Engines: •Subaru WRX with EJ205, EJ207, EJ255 from MY01 to MY05. •Subaru Forester with EJ253 from MY07 to MY09. •Reference: magnetic sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: 15 of 10°, 2 of 30°, 12 of 10°, 1 of 30° (36-2-2-2) per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: end of the single 30° tooth in the 540° pitch tooth on the bank 1 (cylinder 1) inlet camshaft ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: Off ◦Threshold: 0.0V ◦Hysteresis: Engine Speed dependent ◦Offset: 40°. •Inlet Camshafts: magnetic sensor ◦Tooth pattern: 4 even teeth ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: Off ◦Threshold: 0.0V ◦Hysteresis: Engine Speed dependent •Synchronisation: magnetic sensor ◦Tooth pattern: 4-2: 540°, 180° ◦Pattern lock position: start of the 540° pitch camshaft tooth ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: Off ◦Threshold: 0.0V ◦Hysteresis: Engine Speed dependent •SIM3 mode 62. members.name: Ford Coyote
members.doc: Supported Engines: •Ford Coyote (Ford Mustang GT 2011-2017) •Ford Boss 6.2L (F150, F250-550 and Raptor trucks 2011-2016) •Indmar Raptor marine engines Sensor details: •Reference: ◦Tooth pattern: 34 of 10°, 1 of 20° (36-1) per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: wide tooth near the 180° pitch tooth on Bank 1 Inlet Camshaft ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Coyote Offset: 42° ◦Boss 6.2L Offset: 396° •Inlet and Exhaust camshafts: ◦Tooth pattern: 90°, 90°, 120°, 60°, 120°, 60°, 180° (30° tolerance). ◦Pattern lock position: the 180° pitch tooth. ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Bank 1 Inlet Camshaft zero position: 140° ◦Bank 1 Exhaust Camshaft zero position: -30° ◦Bank 2 Inlet Camshaft zero position: -220° ◦Bank 2 Exhaust Camshaft zero position: 330° •Synchronisation: ◦All camshafts used for synchronisation •SIM3 mode: 6F. members.name: PSA EP6DTS
members.doc: Supported Engines: •Prince Engine family from PSA and BMW. Examples: ◦PSA EP6DTS in Peugeot 207 RC/GTI (2006-2010) ◦BMW THP175 in Mini Cooper S Turbo (2007-2010) ◦N18 in Mini R56N •Ford Powerstroke 6.7L Diesel •NOTE: may also support some other engines with an OE Bosch ECU •Reference: hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: 57 of 6°, 1 of 18° (60-2) per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: wide tooth at the 176° active region of the camshaft •Camshaft or Synchronisation: hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: 120°/60°, 184°/120°, 60°/176° (30° tolerance).Both edges used: / indicates the active edge ◦Pattern lock position: end of the wide active region. ◦NOTE: Position will be erratic and Reference Offset will change by 360° if the wrong edge is used. •Prince Engines ◦Reference Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Camshaft Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Reference Offset: 474° •Ford Powerstroke 6.7L Diesel ◦Reference Active Edge: Rising Edge ◦Synchronisation Active Edge: Rising Edge ◦Reference Offset: 126° •SIM3 mode 9D.

members.name: Yamaha FX SHO
members.doc: Yamaha FX SHO engine support for Personal Water Craft. Reference pattern 4+1. The reference cycle lock position is the reference tooth edge between the 15° and 90° pitch teeth. •Reference: Rising edge. •Synchronisation: Falling edge. •Reference Offset: -80°. •SIM3 mode 89. NOTE: Due to the extreme fluctuations in Tooth Period with these engines the following settings should be used: •Narrow Pitch Threshold: 20%. •Wide Pitch Threshold: 200%.

members.name: Subaru FA20D
members.doc: Supported Engines: •EJ257 with single AVCS (MY04-MY05) •EJ205, EJ207, EJ255, EJ257 with dual AVCS (MY06-) •FA20D for BRZ and FT86 (2012-) •Reference: magnetic sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: 15 of 10°, 2 of 30°, 12 of 10°, 1 of 30° (36-2-2-2) per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: end of the single 30° tooth in the 360° pitch tooth on the bank 1 (cylinder 1) inlet camshaft ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: Off ◦Threshold: 0.0V ◦Hysteresis: Engine Speed dependent ◦Offset: 40°. •Inlet Camshafts: ◦Tooth pattern: 4-1: 360°, 180°, 180°. ◦Pattern lock position: start of the first 360° pitch camshaft tooth ◦MY04-MY07: magnetic sensor ◾Active Edge: Falling Edge ◾Pullup Control: Off ◾Threshold: 0.0V ◾Hysteresis: Engine Speed dependent ◦MY08-: hall sensor ◾Active Edge (EJ2xx Bank 1): Rising Edge ◾Active Edge: Falling Edge ◾Pullup Control: On ◾Threshold: 2.0V ◾Hysteresis: 0.5V •Exhaust Camshafts: ◦Tooth pattern: 2 of 360°. ◦MY04-MY07: magnetic sensor ◾Active Edge: Falling Edge ◾Pullup Control: Off ◾Threshold: 0.0V ◾Hysteresis: Engine Speed dependent ◦MY08-: hall sensor ◾Active Edge: Falling Edge ◾Pullup Control: On ◾Threshold: 2.0V ◾Hysteresis: 0.5V •Synchronisation: ◦Both inlet camshafts used for synchronisation •SIM3 mode 73. EJ205, EJ207, EJ255 (MY01-MY05) require the Subaru EJ207AVCS Reference Mode.

members.name: Mitsubishi 4G63T
members.doc: Mitsubishi 4G63T for Lancer Evolution 1-9. Reference teeth 2 of 180° (70° high). The reference cycle lock position is the reference tooth edge that is spanned by the 90° low synchronisation region. •Reference: Rising edge. •Synchronisation: Rising Edge (both edges used). •A camshaft input is supported for measuring MIVEC position. •Reference Offset: 615°. •SIM3 mode 9F.

members.name: Scania DC16
members.doc: Scania DC16 Engine. Reference pattern 60-2, ie 57 teeth of 6° pitch and 1 of 18° pitch. The reference cycle lock position is the end of the 18° pitch tooth before the missing camshaft teeth. •Reference: Falling edge. •Camshaft: Falling edge. •One Synchronisation input supported. •Reference Offset: 0°. •SIM3 mode 9E. Camshaft edge angles are not reported.

members.name: Custom EJ20G
members.doc: Subaru GC8 WRX and STi (EJ20G, EJ20K, EJ207 etc.) from MY95 - MY00with the MY01 crankshaft sprocket (part number 13021AA141). Reference teeth 15 of 10°, 2 of 30°, 12 of 10°, 1 of 30°. Thereference cycle lock position is the end of the single 30° pitch tooth before the double narrow pitch (three tooth) region on the camshaft sprocket. •Reference Offset: 40°. •One synchronisation input supported. •SIM3 mode 74. Camshaft edge angles are reported: •The 'Offset' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used. •The 'Smooth' setting in the camshaft position measurement is not used.

members.name: BMW S62
members.doc: Reference pattern 60-2, ie 57 teeth of 6° pitch and 1 of 18° pitch. The reference cycle lock position is the end of the 18° pitch tooth before the wide exhaust camshaft tooth. Inlet camshaft(s) have 6 evenly spaced teeth. Exhaust camshaft(s)have 8-1 teeth. •BMW E39 M5 S62B50 NOTE: not tested - please contact MoTeCbefore running this engine. •BMW E36 M3 S52B32. •BMW E46 M3 S54B32. •Reference: Falling edge. •Camshaft: Falling edge. •Exhaust camshaft(s) are used for synchronisation. •Reference Offset: 85°. •SIM3 mode 3A.

members.name: Lamborghini V10
members.doc: This mode supports the 5.0L port injected LamborghiniGallardo (approximately 2003 - 2007). This mode should also support the port injectedLamborghini Murciélago. The Reference has 13 teeth of 10°, 1 of 20°, 2 of 10°, 1 of 20°,12 of 10°, 1 of 20°, 1 of 10°, 1 of 20°. The reference cycle lock position is the end of the single 10° pitch tooth before the (approximately) 40°, 40° region on all camshafts. Camshaft inputs use both edges. Active Edge only influences trigger voltages derived from the hysteresis and threshold levels. •Reference: Falling edge. •All camshafts used for synchronisation. •Camshaft pattern lock position: edge between the two 40° wide regions. •Reference Offset: (information required)°. •SIM3 mode 71. The 5.2L direct injected engine (2008 -) requires the Bosch 140 40 Reference Mode. See also the Lamborghini LP520 Reference Mode.

members.name: Dodge Viper
members.doc: This mode supports the Dodge Viper (- 2006). Reference teeth 54°, 18° repeated 5 times. The reference cycle lock position is the end of the 18° pitch tooth before the synchronisation falling edge. •Reference: Falling edge. •Synchronisation: Falling Edge hall sensor (both edges used). •Synchronisation pattern lock position: end of the 360° wide tooth. •Reference Offset: 95°. •SIM3 mode 85.

members.name: Dodge Viper MY2008
members.doc: This mode supports the Dodge Viper (2008 -). Reference pattern 60-2, ie 57 teeth of 6° pitch and 1 of 18° pitch. The reference cycle lock position is the end of the 18° pitch tooth near the 14° wide exhaust camshaft tooth. •Reference: Falling Edge hall sensor. •Exhaust Camshaft: Falling Edge hall sensor (both edges used). •Exhaust camshaft used for synchronisation. •Exhaust camshaft pattern lock position: end of the 14° wide tooth. •Reference Offset: 114°. •SIM3 mode 8B.

members.name: Mitsubishi 6A12
members.doc: This mode supports Mitsubishi V6 engines such as: •6A12 - 2 litre V6 DOHC per bank •6A13 - 2.5 litre V6 SOHC per bank •6G74 - 3.5 litre V6 DOHC per bank with switched MIVEC •6G75 - 3.8 litre V6 SOHC per bank Reference teeth 3 of 120° pitch (70° low) on the crankshaft.synchronisation has 4 teeth in a pattern of 120° and 60° regions. The reference cycle lock position is the reference tooth Falling Edge that is spanned by the 60° low / 60° high synchronisation region. •Reference: Falling edge. •Synchronisation: Falling Edge (both edges used). •Reference Offset: 556°. •SIM3 mode 95.

members.name: Nissan VK56DE
members.doc: Supported Engines: •Nissan VK56DE. •Reference: hall sensor. ◦Tooth pattern: (36-1-1) 16 of 10°, 1 of 20° twice per revolution ◦Cycle lock position: end of the single 20° near the start of the synchronisation pattern ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 2.5V ◦Hysteresis: 1.0V ◦Offset: 230°. •Inlet Camshafts: hall sensor ◦Tooth pattern: 4 even teeth ◦Active Edge: Falling Edge ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 2.5V ◦Hysteresis: 1.0V ◦Bank 1 zero position: 50° ◦Bank 2 zero position: 140° •Synchronisation: hall sensor ◦Tooth pattern: 10 teeth in groups of 1, 3, 4 and 2 with 30° pitch ◦Pattern lock position: Rising Edge of the single tooth ◦Active Edge: Rising Edge ◦Pullup Control: On ◦Threshold: 1.5V ◦Hysteresis: 1.0V •SIM3 mode 8F.

members.name: Toyota 2ZZ
members.doc: Toyota 2ZZ, 3GS and others with VVT. Reference pattern 36-2, ie 33 teeth of 10° pitch and 1 of 30° pitch. Thereference cycle lock position is the reference tooth edge at the start of the 360° pitch tooth on the bank 1 (cylinder 1) inlet camshaft. The inlet and exhaust camshafts have three teeth in a 4-1 pattern. •Reference: Falling Edge magnetic sensor. •Synchronisation: all camshafts used for synchronisation. •Camshafts: Falling Edge magnetic sensor. •Camshaft pattern lock position: start of the 360° pitch tooth. •Reference Offset: 550°. •SIM3 mode 39.

members.name: Subaru FA20 DIT
members.doc: Subaru FA20DIT. Reference teeth 14 of 10°, 1 of 40°, 15 of 10°, 1 of 30°. Thereference cycle lock position is the reference tooth edge at the start of the 360° pitch tooth on the bank 1 (cylinder 1) inlet camshaft. Inlet camshafts have three teeth in a 4-1 pattern.Exhaust camshafts have two teeth. •Reference: Rising Edge hall sensor. •Synchronisation: all camshafts used for synchronisation. •Camshafts: Rising Edge hall sensor. •Inlet camshaft pattern lock position: start of the 360° pitch tooth. •Reference Offset: 30°. •SIM3 mode 75.

members.name: Toyota 2JZ GE
members.doc: Toyota 6 cylinder 2JZ-GE with VVT (eg Lexus IS300). Reference pattern 36-2, ie 33 teeth of 10° pitch and 1 of 30° pitch. Thereference cycle lock position is the end of the missing tooth which has the closest inlet camshaft tooth. The inlet camshaft has three teeth evenly spaced teeth. •Reference: Falling Edge magnetic sensor. •Synchronisation: inlet camshaft used for synchronisation. •Inlet Camshaft: Falling Edge magnetic sensor. •Camshaft pattern lock position: the edge closest to the missing tooth. •Reference Offset: 520°. •SIM3 mode 3E.

members.name: Mazda RX8
members.doc: Mazda RENESIS 13B-MSP (eg Mazda RX8). Reference teeth 15 of 10°, 2 of 30°, 12 of 10°, 1 of 30°. Thereference cycle lock position is the end of the single 30° pitch tooth. •Reference: Falling Edge magnetic sensor. •Synchronisation: not required. •Reference Offset 35°. •SIM3 mode 6D.

members.name: Mitsubishi 4B11
members.doc: Mitsubishi 4B11 (eg Lancer Evolution X). Reference teeth 16 of 10°, 1 of 20°, 15 of 10°, 1 of 30°. members.name: Lamborghini LP520
members.doc: This mode supports the 5.0L port injected LamborghiniGallardo LP520 GT3 (approximately 2006 - 2008). The Reference has 13 teeth of 10°, 1 of 20°, 2 of 10°, 1 of 20°,5 of 10°, 1 of 20°, 5 of 10°, 1 of 20°, 1 of 10°, 1 of 20°. The reference cycle lock position is the end of the single 10° pitch tooth before the (approximately) 40°, 40° region on all camshafts. Camshaft inputs use both edges. Active Edge only influences trigger voltages derived from the hysteresis and threshold levels. •Reference: Falling edge. •All camshafts used for synchronisation. •Camshaft pattern lock position: edge between the two 40° wide regions. •Reference Offset: (information required)°. The 5.2L direct injected engine (2008 -) requires the Bosch 140 40 Reference Mode. See also the Lamborghini V10 Reference Mode.

members.name: Batch Fire
members.doc: Batch Fire mode allows Fuel and Ignition outputs to operate as soon as possible. This allows starting the engine: •Faster: with many Reference Mode. •Without a synchronisation sensor. •With a faulty synchronisation sensor. When Reference is operating in Batch Fire mode State will report either: •Cylinder Lock: When the ignition system is setup for a distributor and the Reference pattern repeats once for each of the Cylinders.Fuel timing may be offset by a multiple of (Cycle Duration / Cylinders)°. •Revolution Lock: the Reference pattern repeats once per engine revolution.Fuel timing may be offset by 360°. NOTE: Batch Fire mode not recommended for direct injection or diesel engines.This is because fuel output timing may be offset.

members.name: Sequential
members.doc: Full sequential operation. Fuel and ignition outputs are only driven when State is Cycle Lock. This was the only starting mode supported in M1 Systems before 1.4.0.88.

members.doc: Engine speed is being simulated as specified by Test Speed.

members.name: Cylinder Lock
members.doc: Locked to the engine cylinder. Engine synchronous pulses such Fuel and Ignition can be generated but are not synchronised with the correct cylinder. Only used when: •Start Mode is Batch Fire •Reference pattern repeats once per cylinder. One example of this is one tooth per firing •The ignition system is setup for a distributor

members.name: Revolution Lock
members.doc: Locked to the engine revolution. Engine synchronous pulses such as Fuel and Ignition can be generated but will not be synchronised with the correct cylinder. Only used when: •Start Mode is Batch Fire •Reference pattern repeats once per engine revolution. One example of this is a "missing tooth" mode. Individual coil ignition systems will generate an additional Wasted Spark: one spark per engine revolution.

members.name: Rejected Pulse

Check the sensor installation and view the sensor waveform with Online->Capture Inputs.

members.doc: The reported period is 0 or greater than Timeout.

members.name: Sensor Fallback
members.doc: No rotational speed measurement for Drive is available. Drive is calculated using available wheel sensor(s).

members.name: Vehicle Speed Fallback
members.doc: No rotational speed sensor is available. Drive is calculated based on Vehicle Speed.

classes.Absolute Pressure: Use this class for linear absolute pressure sensors. Predefined sensor calibrations can be selected in Calibration. Alternatively the Translation table can be used which converts the sensor voltage into a calibrated signal.
classes.Absolute Pressure Ext: Use this class for linear absolute pressure sensors. Predefined sensor calibrations can be selected in Calibration. Alternatively the Translation table can be used which converts the sensor voltage into a calibrated signal.
classes.Absolute Voltage: This low-level hardware interface class provides an absolute voltage measurement with digital filtering. This class is primarily for diagnostic purposes: the 'Ratiometric Voltage' class should be used forsensors.
classes.Acceleration: Use this class for linear acceleration sensors where a simple scale and offset are appropriate.
classes.Acceleration Ext: Use this class for linear acceleration sensors where a simple scale and offset are appropriate.
classes.Alternate Reference: This low-level hardware interface class provides alternate engine speed reference inputs for additional, redundant, crank angle sensors.
classes.Boost Control: This class implements control of turbocharger boost with a pulsed solenoid. It is recommended that this be named 'Boost' when added to a project.
classes.Boost Control with Servo: This class implements control of turbocharger boost with a pulsed solenoid or servo motor. It is recommended that this be named 'Boost' when added to a project. This combines Boost and Boost Servo
classes.Bridge Drive: Pulse width modulation control of a full bridge output.
classes.Cam Pump: Controls a mechanical high pressure pump for direct injection applications.
classes.Camshaft: This low-level hardware interface class implements camshaft position measurement and synchronises the crankshaft position with the engine cycle.
classes.Camshaft Phase: This class implements control of camshaft phase for a single camshaft.
classes.CAN Block Receive: This class manages the CAN block receive subsystem.
classes.CAN Bus: This class manages a CAN bus.
classes.CAN Custom Message Transmit: This class provides the interface structure for the ECU Transmit Custom feature. Extra scripts are required within the main project to make use of this class.
classes.Channel: A channel represents a single value written by the M1 ECU firmware. The value of a channel can be viewed but not changed by M1 Tune.
classes.Constant:
A constant is a single value that cannot be viewed, logged or changed from within code or in M1 Tune.
A constant can be used to store a floating point, integer or enumerated value.
classes.Decode: This low-level hardware interface class decodes a variety of waveforms on a digital input. Itdoes not directly output the values, instead provides data via additional ratiometric resources.
classes.Dual Boost Control: This class implements control of twin turbocharger boost with a pulsed solenoid per bank. It is recommended that thisbe named 'Boost' when added to a project.
classes.Dual Driven Wheel Speed: Use this class for wheel speed measurement on an axle that may contain a differential. This classsupports direct sensor measurement of wheel and differential speeds and calculates slip.
classes.Dual Driven Wheel Speed Ext: Use this class for wheel speed measurement on an axle that may contain a differential. This class supports direct sensor measurement of wheel and differential speeds and calculates slip.
classes.Dual Rate Event:
This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware with a configurable period.
The event can be triggered at either its 'Default Rate' or 'Idle Rate' periodic rate.
classes.Dual Sensor Boost Control: This class implements control of turbocharger boost with a pulsed solenoid. It is recommended that this be named 'Boost' when added to a project.
classes.Dual Throttle Servo:
This class implements a complete throttle servo incorporating: 
•position sensor
•control loop
•output
•diagnostics
classes.Dual Wheel Speed: Use this class for wheel speed measurement on an axle without a differential. This class supports direct sensor measurement of wheel speeds and calculates slip.
classes.Edge: This low-level hardware interface class measures the Active Edge of a digital input.
classes.Fuel Composition: Use this class for fuel composition sensors which require calibration. Predefined sensor calibrations canbe selected in calibration. Alternatively the Translation table can be used which convertsthe sensor input into a calibrated signal. In case of sensor faults the value will be set to NAN (not anumber).
classes.Fuel Pressure: Use this class for fuel pressure sensors. Supported sensor types are absolute pressure sensors, gauge pressure sensors, sealed gauge pressure sensors, differential pressure sensors and gauge pressure resistive sensors. Predefined sensor calibrations can be selected in Calibration. Alternatively the Translation table can be used which converts the sensor voltage into a calibrated signal.
classes.Fuel Pump Relay: This class controls a fuel pump relay output.
classes.Function:
A custom function that executes in the M1.
A function is a container for M1 Programming Language code. This code can be used for logic andcalculations in a project.
classes.GPS: This class receives and decodes data from a NMEA 0183 compliant GPS receiver.
classes.Group: A group can be used to store other objects as children.
classes.Hybrid Sensor: Use this class for wired sensors which require calibration. A table converts the sensor voltage into acalibrated signal. A default value can be defined if the sensor is faulty or not active.
classes.Hybrid Sensor Ext: Use this class for wired sensors which require calibration. A table converts the sensor voltage into a calibrated signal. A default value can be defined if the sensor is faulty or not active.
classes.Ignition: This low-level hardware interface generates the output waveform for a single ignition coil.
classes.Incremental Encoder: This low-level hardware interface class implements incremental rotary encoder measurement.
classes.Indexed Nine Position Switch: A flexible switch which allows allocation of an external switch as input, and assumes the value of theallocated switch. The output uses the enumerated 'Nine Position Switch State Enumeration' data type.
classes.Indexed Switch: A flexible switch that allows allocation of an external switch as input, and generates an outputdependant upon the switch value. The data type of the output values can be chosen.
classes.Internal Temperature: Use this class to measure the internal ECU temperature. This class supports an absolute voltage measurement with a fixed conversion into a temperature matching the M1 Internal Temperature voltage input.
classes.IO Resource Constant:
A resource constant is a resource value that cannot be viewed, logged or changed from within M1 Tuneand cannot be modified once the project has been compiled.
The selected resource cannot be re-used by any other resource object.
classes.IO Resource Parameter:
A resource parameter represents a resource value that can be viewed and configured by M1 Tune.
M1 Tune will ensure that the value of a resource parameter is unique, and is not re-used by any other resource parameters or constants.
classes.Knock Window: This class generates the engine knock measurement window. Only one 'Knock Window' class is supported. Both internal DSP knock measurement and external knock measurement (with a MoTeC SKM or MoTeCOEK) are supported.
classes.Lap Time: This class implements a lap time calculation using a MoTeC BR2, switched inputs or a GPS.
classes.Latchable Switch: Use this class for two position switches or buttons. The class translates the switch voltage into a stateor reads the edge to toggle the state.
classes.LIN Bus: This low-level hardware interface class configures flexible IO to operate as a serial port.
classes.Linear: Use this class for linear sensors where a simple scale and offset are appropriate.
classes.Linear Ext: Use this class for linear sensors where a simple scale and offset are appropriate.
classes.Low Side Ignition Group: This low-level interface class manages global hardware for all low side ignition output pins providing configuration and diagnostic reporting.
classes.LTC: This class receives and decodes CAN data from a MoTeC LTC, LTCN or PLM.
classes.Mass Air Flow: Use this class for mass air flow sensors which require calibration. Predefined sensor calibrations can be selected in Calibration. Alternatively the Translation table can be used which converts the sensor voltage into a calibrated signal. In case of sensor faults the value will be set to NAN (not a number).
classes.Minimum Maximum Warning: This class implements a generic minimum/maximum warning. A defined input value is monitored in terms of a minimum and maximum threshold. A warning is generated and an engine speed limit can be applied if thethresholds are exceeded.
classes.Minimum Maximum Warning with Limits: This class implements a generic minimum/maximum warning. A defined input value is monitored in terms of a minimum and maximum threshold. A warning is generated and an engine speed limit and an air flow limit (Throttle Limit or Torque Limit) can be applied if the thresholds are exceeded.
classes.Multi Position Switch: Use this class for multi position rotary switches (0 up to position 9 as defined by the base value data type). The class translates an input voltage into an enumeration output defined by the user. This enumeration must have 'Default' as index -1 followed by the positions indexed from 0 up to 9 (any name can be used).
classes.Nine Position Switch: Use this class for nine position rotary switches. The class translates an input voltage into a 'Nine Position Switch State Enumeration' output.
classes.Normalised: Use this class for relative position sensors with one feedback channel. This input type is preferred for servo feedback channels as it reports linear values beyond 0.0% and 100.0%.
classes.Normalised Tracking: Use this class for relative position sensors with two feedback channels (Main and Tracking). This input type is preferred for servo feedback channels as it reports linear values beyond 0.0% and 100.0%.
classes.On 1000Hz: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.On 100Hz: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.On 10Hz: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.On 1Hz: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.On 200Hz: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.On 20Hz: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.On 2Hz: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.On 500Hz: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.On 50Hz: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.On 5Hz: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.On Shutdown: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.On Startup: This class provides a trigger for performing an action in an M1 firmware.
classes.Parameter: A parameter represents a single value that can be viewed and changed by M1 Tune. It cannot be changed in code.
classes.PDM: This class receives and decodes CAN data from a MoTeC Power Distribution Module (PDM).
classes.Peak Hold Injector: This low-level interface class manages the hardware for a single peak hold injector output pin providing a configuration and diagnostic reporting.
classes.Peak Hold Injector Group: This low-level interface class manages global hardware for all peak hold injector output pins providing configuration and diagnostic reporting.
classes.Peak Hold Modulation:
This low-level hardware interface class generates an advanced form of pulse width modulation which adds the following features:
•Start Delay: delay before the output starts to operate, toallow precise alignment of a series of output pulses on separateoutputs.
•Peak / Hold control: management of the initial peak pulse prior topulse width modulation to allow management of the current ininductive loads.
•Overall width control: for generation of a precise overall pulse duration.
classes.Periodic Input: This low-level hardware interface class implements frequency, duty cycle and pulse width measurement between Active Edges of the waveform on a digital input.
classes.Port Injection: This low-level hardware interface generates the output waveform for a single fuel injector.
classes.Pressure: Use this class for linear pressure sensors. Supported sensor types are absolute pressure sensors, gauge pressure sensors, sealed gauge pressure sensors, differential pressure sensors and gauge pressure resistive sensors. Predefined sensor calibrations can be selected in Calibration. Alternatively the Translation table can be used which converts the sensor voltage into a calibrated signal.
classes.Pressure Ext: Use this class for linear pressure sensors. Supported sensor types are absolute pressure sensors, gauge pressure sensors, sealed gauge pressure sensors, differential pressure sensors and gauge pressure resistive sensors. Predefined sensor calibrations can be selected in Calibration. Alternatively the Translation table can be used which converts the sensor voltage into a calibrated signal.
classes.Proportional Valve Direct Injection Pump: This class implements control of proportional valve direct injection pumps such as the Bosch CP3.3 diesel pump.
This class implements control of proportional valve direct injection pumps with a single drive output (i.e. not bridge drive).
classes.Pulse Buffer: This low-level hardware interface class implements pulse angle and width measurement between active edges for up to 10 pulses in the engine cycle.
classes.Pulse Width Modulation: This low-level hardware interface class generates pulse width modulation.
classes.PWM Output: This class controls a simple PWM output.
classes.Ratiometric Voltage: This low-level hardware interface class provides either absolute orratiometric voltage measurement with digital filtering. This class isprovided for sensor measurements as the output voltage from many sensors is proportional to the supply voltage.
classes.Reference: This low-level hardware interface class implements engine speed measurement and calculates the engine angle. This measurement interprets the pattern of the waveform from the crank angle sensor.
classes.Scheduled Function:
A custom function that executes in the M1.
A function is a container for M1 Programming Language code. This code can be used for logic andcalculations in a project.
classes.Sensor: Use this class for sensors which require calibration. A table converts the sensor voltage into acalibrated signal. In case of sensor faults the value will be set to NAN (not a number).
classes.Sensor Ext: Use this class for sensors which require calibration. A table converts the sensor voltage into a calibrated signal. In case of sensor faults the value will be set to NAN (not a number).
classes.Serial Interface: This low-level hardware interface class configures flexible IO to operate as a serial port.
classes.Serial Port: This low-level hardware interface class configures flexible IO to operate as a serial port.
classes.Servo Motor:
This class implements a generic servo control, incorporating:
•position sensor
•control loop
•output
•diagnostics
Features include PID control with configurable feed forward component, configurable diagnostic conditions, arrangement for position offset calibration strategy, an engine speed limit parameter for diagnostic faults. 
A method must be called for this class to operate. Refer to Method below for a code example.
classes.Single BMW Throttle Servo:
This class implements a complete throttle servo incorporating: 
•position sensor
•control loop
•output
•diagnostics
classes.Single Driven Wheel Speed: Use this class for wheel speed measurement on a single driven wheel, for example the rear wheel of amotorcycle. This class supports direct sensor measurements of wheel and drive speeds and calculates slip.
classes.Single Throttle Servo:
This class implements a complete throttle servo incorporating: 
•position sensor
•control loop
•output
•diagnostics
classes.Single Wheel Speed: Use this class for wheel speed measurement on a single wheel, for example the front wheel of amotorcycle. This class supports direct sensor measurement of wheel speed and calculates slip.
classes.Slave: This low-level hardware interface class allows a 'Slave' output to becontrolled by a 'Master' input.
classes.Switched Output: This class controls a switched output.
classes.Synchronous Direct Injection Pump: This class implements control of synchronous direct injection pumps such as the Bosch HPD5, various pumpsfrom Denso and both Hitachi types: normally open (Gen I) and normally closed (Gen III).
classes.Table: A table is a matrix of values which can be configured with 1, 2 or 3 axes. Each configured axis is allocated an object which is used to perform an interpolated look up on the matrix.
classes.Temperature: Use this class for temperature sensors which require calibration. Predefined sensor calibrations can beselected in Calibration. Alternatively the Translation table can be used which converts the sensor voltage into a calibrated signal. In case of sensor faults the value will be set to NAN (not anumber).
classes.Temperature Ext: Use this class for temperature sensors which require calibration. Predefined sensor calibrations can be selected in Calibration. Alternatively the Translation table can be used which converts the sensor voltage into a calibrated signal. In case of sensor faults the value will be set to NAN (not a number).
classes.Throttle Pedal: Use this class for linear throttle pedal sensors with two feedback channels (Main and Tracking ).
classes.Throttle Position: Use this class for linear throttle position sensors where a simple scale and offset are appropriate. Theoutput of this class is a ratio between 0 and 100%.
classes.Timer: This class provides a countdown timer which may be used within code to effect time-outs and other functions. The accuracy of the timer is high, but the effectiveness of its application is dependent upon the execution rate of the code from which it is monitored.
classes.Tuning Method: Tuning methods provide firmware interaction with M1 Tune to allow table or parameter values to be updated. The tuning method class is only executed upon demand. When the 'Q' key is pressed in M1 Tune the current input value is sampled and the method is executed.
classes.Turbocharger Speed: Use this class for rotational speed measurement on a rotor shaft of a turbocharger.
classes.UDIG Voltages: This low-level hardware interface class provides dedicated diagnostic voltagemeasurements for Universal Digital Input pins.
classes.Universal Digital Input: This low-level interface class manages the hardware for a single 'Universal Digital Input' pin providing configuration and diagnostic reporting.
classes.Universal Switch: This low-level hardware interface class measures the state of a switch connected to a wide range of inputs.
classes.Voltage: Use this class to measure an absolute voltage. In addition, the class provides diagnostic information about the quality of the input.
